page: Amazon Web Services r3.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the Xen hypervisor, this instance provides dedicated CPU allocation with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 61.0 GB of system memory, translating to 7.62 GB per core, and includes 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates moderate performance characteristics. The instance achieves average results in Passmark CPU and memory tests, as well as average throughput in static web serving and Geekbench software compilation. However, single-core processing capabilities are limited, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the weak and poor categories respectively. Memory bandwidth remains steady in the average tier for both small and large block read/write operations. Cryptographic performance is supported by hardware-accelerated AES, though the lack of AVX-512 and SHA-NI extensions limits efficiency in modern vector and hashing workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r3.2xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and bundled local NVMe storage, which reduces the need for external storage attachments for temporary data. However, the older Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 architecture presents a tradeoff, as its raw compute and single-threaded performance are significantly lower than newer generation hardware. This makes the instance less suitable for CPU-bound tasks or real-time LLM inference, where text generation speeds for a 7B model drop to a poor rating.

This instance is configured for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute power. Suitable applications include memory-intensive database operations, caching layers, and legacy enterprise applications that benefit from dedicated vCPUs and local NVMe storage but do not demand high-frequency single-core execution.
description: Amazon Web Services r3.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 4 physical Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 cores running at 2.5 GHz, and 61.0 GB of RAM. It includes 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Xen hypervisor, the instance delivers dedicated CPU allocation. Benchmark results show average performance in multi-core CPU tests, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core execution and modern vector calculations are limited due to the older processor generation and lack of AVX-512 support. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes this instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database caching, and legacy enterprise applications that require a balance of high memory capacity and local NVMe storage without demanding high single-threaded compute performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r3.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 61.0 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores, substantial RAM capacity, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage.

61.0 GB of system memory providing 7.62 GB per core
160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency temporary data

Suited for memory-intensive caching, legacy databases, and enterprise applications
